title: NOC 11102 – Financial Advisors | GTR Immigration
description: Financial Advisors classified under NOC 11102 are among the most sought-after finance professionals in Canada’s immigration system. These specialists guide individuals and families on investments, retirement, insurance, and wealth management—making them strong candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Professionals in this occupation may perform some or all of the following:
Develop comprehensive financial plans covering cash flow, insurance, investments, retirement, estate planning, and taxation
Analyze client financial records and define objectives
Recommend and help implement tailored financial strategies
Arrange purchases and sales of financial products (subject to licensing)
Monitor investment portfolios for performance and risk
Expand business networks and attract new clients
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in business administration, commerce, economics, or a related field
Certified Financial Planner (CFP) designation may be required
Provincial licensing to sell regulated products such as RRSPs, annuities, and life insurance
Mandatory certification as a Financial Planner in Quebec
Training programs through recognized financial institutes and organizations may be required by employers
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is expected to generate 66,000 new positions for Financial Advisors, while approximately 61,400 job seekers may enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—especially applicants from India—this highlights strong long-term employment prospects when supported by a well-planned immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Financial Advisors remain in high demand across Canada’s banking, insurance, and investment sectors. Post-pandemic economic growth and increased retirement-planning needs have further strengthened hiring trends.
Excellent immigration opportunities for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings commonly range from $41,600 to $120,300, depending on experience, province, and specialization.
Professionals immigrating under NOC 11102 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
